title: get_list opcode — list destructuring
purpose: The `get_list` opcode (opcode 65) destructures a cons cell into its head and tail in a single instruction. It is the inverse of `put_list` and is emitted by the compiler for virtually every list pattern match (`[H|T]`). Without it, any Gleam or Erlang code that pattern-matches on lists fails with `UnsupportedOpcode(65)`. This is the single most blocking missing opcode — it appears in nearly every compiled module.
task: Add a dedicated `Instruction::GetList` variant to the loader decoder (currently decoded as `Generic`), wire it into the opcode dispatch table, and implement the handler in `guards.rs` alongside the existing `get_hd` and `get_tl` opcodes.

requirements.title: Loader decode — dedicated Instruction variant
requirements.spec: Replace the `Generic { opcode: 65, name: "get_list", operands }` decode path with a dedicated `Instruction::GetList { source, head, tail }` variant.
acceptance: `Instruction::GetList` variant exists with `source: Operand`, `head: Operand`, `tail: Operand`
Existing .beam files that use `get_list` load without errors
Tests verify the decode produces the correct variant

requirements.title: Opcode dispatch wiring
requirements.spec: Add `Instruction::GetList` to the dispatch table in `opcodes/mod.rs`, routing to a handler in `guards.rs`.
acceptance: `Instruction::GetList` is matched in the dispatch table
No `UnsupportedOpcode(65)` error when executing modules containing `get_list`

requirements.title: get_list handler implementation
requirements.spec: Implement `get_list` in `guards.rs`. The instruction reads a cons cell from the source operand, writes the head to the head destination and the tail to the tail destination.
acceptance: `get_list(source, head_dest, tail_dest)` extracts head and tail from a cons cell
Source is read via `core::read_term()`, validated as a cons via `Cons::new()`
Destinations can be X or Y registers
Test: `get_list` on `[1, 2, 3]` writes `1` to head and `[2, 3]` to tail
Test: `get_list` on a single-element list `[42]` writes `42` to head and `NIL` to tail

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the cons cell heap representation — use the existing `Cons` accessor from `term/boxed/accessors.rs`
SHALL NOT implement `get_list` as two separate `get_hd` + `get_tl` calls — it is a single atomic instruction
SHALL NOT add branching/failure semantics — `get_list` on a non-list is a badarg (not a branch to fail label)
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
